summary: Assemble ONE property's full evidence ledger -- distress signal, owner identity, valuation (ARV/MAO), buyer demand, seller price evidence, assignment spread, and queue decision -- each tagged with the module/source that produced it, plus a buyer-safe redacted view.

files.role: buildProofStack() (internal) + buyerSafeProofStack() (redacted) -- pure assembly of one property's evidence.
rankBuyerDemand() supplies the buyer-demand layer of the stack.
evaluateWholesaleSpread() supplies the spread + buyer-acceptance layer.
buildPropertyKgEvidenceView(): KG-backed evidence view used by the kg evidence route.
Evidence-preserving field edges underpinning proof provenance.

routes.summary: Full internal proof stack for a property id (buildProofStack).
Buyer-safe redacted proof stack (buyerSafeProofStack) -- hides competing buyer names.
KG-backed evidence view for a property.
Raw property_evidence rows backing the stack.

tests.role: Unit tests for buildProofStack and buyerSafeProofStack.

tables.name: properties
tables.role: Base record the stack is built for.

tables.name: property_evidence
tables.role: Stored evidence rows surfaced in the stack.

tables.name: seller_price_evidence
tables.role: Seller price layer of the stack.

docs.role: Defines 'Proof stack per property' (#5) and the Evidence Ledger module that proof_stack.js implements.

audit_findings.finding: Proof-stack APIs and buyer-safe proof-stack views are cited as real quality signals; the marketplace view should make the relationship between buyer demand and property proof more visible (what proof supports the deal, what is still uncertain).
Proof stack is covered by the test/quality gate suite.

next_actions.action: Show evidence completeness and 'next missing field' per property so the stack doubles as a checklist of what to gather next.
Make 'what is redacted' explicit in the buyer-safe view so buyers see the boundary, not a silent omission.
